scaled_oracle_cases.description: Transformed seed case covering all scaled operators: non-default separators, extra fields, multi-character separator, leading/trailing separators.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that get_cell_umi_read_string handles various separator types, extra fields, multi-character separators, and leading/trailing separators returning the correct UMI and cell barcode bytes.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: For any string separator (single or multi-character), extract the last two fields as bytes. If the read_id has fewer than two separators, raise ValueError. Leading/trailing separators produce empty fields.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old solution may fail on non-default separators, extra fields, multi-character separators, or leading/trailing separators.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
read_id_good = 'NB551068:51:H3JTYBGXX:1:11101:10000:1001_CB10123_UMIACTGGA'
read_id_bad = 'NB551068:51:H3JTYBGXX:1:11101:10000:1001'
read_id_dash = 'NB551068:51:H3JTYBGXX:1:11101:10000:1001-CB10123-UMIACTGGA'
read_id_colon = 'NB551068:51:H3JTYBGXX:1:11101:10000:1001:CB10123:UMIACTGGA'
read_id_extra = 'NB551068:51:H3JTYBGXX:1:11101:10000:1001_extra0_CB10123_UMIACTGGA'
read_id_double_underscore = 'NB551068:51:H3JTYBGXX:1:11101:10000:1001__CB10123__UMIACTGGA'
read_id_leading = '_CB10123_UMIACTGGA'
read_id_trailing = 'CB10123_UMIACTGGA_'
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
umi, cell_barcode = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id_good, '_')
print("Good read -> UMI bytes:", umi, "Cell barcode bytes:", cell_barcode)
try:
    get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id_bad, '_')
except ValueError as e:
    print("Expected error for bad read ID:", e)
umi2, cb2 = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id_dash, '-')
print("Dash separator -> UMI bytes:", umi2, "Cell barcode bytes:", cb2)
umi3, cb3 = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id_colon, ':')
print("Colon separator -> UMI bytes:", umi3, "Cell barcode bytes:", cb3)
umi4, cb4 = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id_extra, '_')
print("Extra fields -> UMI bytes:", umi4, "Cell barcode bytes:", cb4)
umi5, cb5 = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id_double_underscore, '__')
print("Double underscore -> UMI bytes:", umi5, "Cell barcode bytes:", cb5)
umi6, cb6 = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id_leading, '_')
print("Leading sep -> UMI bytes:", umi6, "Cell barcode bytes:", cb6)
umi7, cb7 = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id_trailing, '_')
print("Trailing sep -> UMI bytes:", umi7, "Cell barcode bytes:", cb7)
result = {'umi': umi, 'cell_barcode': cell_barcode}
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include tests with non-default separators.
Scaled executable cases must include a read ID with more than two fields.
Scaled executable cases must include a test with a multi-character separator.
Scaled executable cases must include read IDs with leading and trailing separators.

stdout_contains: Good read -> UMI bytes: b'UMIACTGGA' Cell barcode bytes: b'CB10123'
